title: Serverless - Event-Driven Architecture on AWS
description: Design a highly scalable, decoupled event flow utilizing Lambda, SQS, SNS, and EventBridge.
body:
Design a robust serverless architecture on AWS for [BUSINESS_PROCESS].

<context>
Requirements:
- Invocation Trigger: [HTTP API / S3 Upload / Cron / External Event]
- Core Processing: [Business Logic Summary]
- External Integrations: [Third-party Services / Databases]
- Fault Tolerance: Retry mechanisms and DLQ routing.
</context>

<task>
Architect the solution focusing on these components:
1. **AWS Lambda**: Define precise memory allocations and timeout thresholds.
2. **Messaging**: Analyze and justify the use of SQS vs SNS vs EventBridge for decoupling.
3. **API Gateway**: Formulate Auth layers and traffic throttling rules.
4. **IaC**: Generate the infrastructure scaffold in [Terraform / AWS CDK / SAM].

Provide:
- A detailed logical data flow sequence.
- The Infrastructure as Code (IaC) skeleton.
- A comprehensive observability strategy (CloudWatch / X-Ray traces).
- A baseline architectural cost estimation.
</task>
author: community

title: Terraform - Multi-Cloud Abstraction Modules
description: Create a unified Terraform abstraction layer capable of deploying parallel infrastructure across AWS and Azure.
body:
Act as a Principal Cloud DevOps Architect.

<task>
Design a polymorphic Terraform module that abstracts the creation of a foundational Network (VPC/VNet) and a Kubernetes Cluster seamlessly across both AWS and Azure.

Module Structure Requirements:
- **Conditional Execution**: Utilize advanced conditional expressions (`count` / `for_each`) to dynamically enable or disable target cloud providers.
- **Standardization**: Enforce a unified tagging strategy and standardized output schema regardless of the active provider.
- **State Management**: Configure robust remote state handling utilizing centralized S3 or Blob Storage with lock mechanics.

Provide:
- The abstracted module code.
- An example `main.tf` demonstrating how to invoke the module dynamically via environment variables.
</task>
author: community

title: FinOps - Cloud Cost Auditing and Optimization
description: Identify infrastructure waste and formulate an immediate, actionable cost reduction strategy.
body:
Act as a Cloud Financial Operations (FinOps) Engineer.

<context>
Analyze the following high-level billing report from [AWS / GCP / Azure]:
[INSERT_EXPENSE_DETAILS]
</context>

<task>
Execute a cost-optimization audit targeting the following objectives:
1. Pinpoint 'idle', unattached, or severely over-provisioned compute and storage resources.
2. Recommend a strategic shift towards Spot Instances or Savings Plans where statistically viable.
3. Propose aggressive lifecycle policies for object storage (e.g., S3 Standard to Glacier Deep Archive).
4. Calculate an estimated percentage of total potential savings post-optimization.
</task>
author: community

title: Kubernetes - GitOps Workflow Initialization
description: Implement a continuous deployment architecture driven strictly by Git state for production clusters.
body:
Architect a complete GitOps deployment pipeline for a production-grade Kubernetes application.

<context>
Target Stack:
- GitOps Controller: [ArgoCD / FluxCD]
- Manifest Templating: [Helm / Kustomize]
</context>

<task>
Generate the foundational YAML configurations to deploy this system:
1. Formulate the `Application` Custom Resource Definition (CRD) for ArgoCD or the equivalent GitRepository/Kustomization for Flux.
2. Configure explicit auto-synchronization rules and resource pruning policies.
3. Define the automated rollback strategy triggered by failing health/liveness probes.
4. Document the authentication integration establishing a secure tunnel between the cluster and a private GitHub/GitLab repository.
</task>
author: community

title: Disaster Recovery - Business Continuity Plan 2026
description: Design a highly resilient disaster recovery architecture ensuring minimal RTO and RPO limits.
body:
Formulate a strict Disaster Recovery (DR) strategy for a mission-critical [SYSTEM_TYPE].

<task>
Evaluate the following architectural methodologies and select the most appropriate given the system type:
- Backup & Restore
- Pilot Light
- Warm Standby
- Multi-region Active-Active

Provide the implementation blueprint containing:
1. Database cross-region replication configuration.
2. An automated DNS failover/traffic routing script using Route53/Traffic Manager.
3. A Post-Incident verification checklist to confirm data integrity.
4. A theoretical schedule and protocol for bi-annual DR mock failover drills.
</task>
author: community

title: Edge Computing - Global Content and Compute Distribution
description: Utilize Cloudfront Functions or Edge Workers to execute compute logic physically closer to the end user.
body:
Design a low-latency edge architecture to reduce Time-To-First-Byte (TTFB) for users distributed across [GLOBAL_REGION].

<task>
Write the Edge Function implementation in [Cloudfront Functions / AWS Lambda@Edge / Cloudflare Workers] that executes the following:
1. Dynamic request routing and URL rewriting based on inbound geolocation headers.
2. Stateless JWT validation at the edge layer, dropping unauthorized requests before they hit the origin server.
3. Logic to intelligently cache and assemble dynamic HTML fragments to drastically reduce backend compute loads.
</task>
author: community

title: IAM - Zero Trust and Principle of Least Privilege
description: Engineer highly granular access policies limiting cloud service and lateral user movement.
body:
Construct a strict, JSON-formatted Identity and Access Management (IAM) Policy.

<task>
The policy must explicitly grant ONLY the following permissions:
- Read-only access (`GetObject`, `ListBucket`) strictly to the S3 bucket named 'public-assets'.
- Write-only access (`PutObject`) strictly to the S3 bucket named 'user-uploads'.
- The ability to specifically restart (`ec2:RebootInstances`) ONLY the EC2 instance with the precise ID: [INSTANCE_ID].

<constraints>
Ensure the policy utilizes conditional statements (`Condition` blocks) restricting these actions to a specific IP CIDR block and a defined business hours time window to maximize security.
</constraints>
author: community
